payload.formula_text:
Lift = ((Winner Rate - Loser Rate) / Loser Rate) x 100
Z-score = (p1 - p2) / sqrt(p_pooled x (1 - p_pooled) x (1/n1 + 1/n2))
payload.formula_explanation: The lift percentage shows how much better the winning variant performed. Statistical significance is calculated using a Z-test, where 95% confidence (Z > 1.96) indicates a reliable winner.
how_to_read: Green banner highlights the winning variant with lift percentage
Compare subject lines side-by-side to understand what worked
Look at Open Rate to evaluate subject line effectiveness
Look at Click Rate to evaluate CTA and content effectiveness
CTOR helps isolate content performance from subject line impact
decisions_answered: Which variant performed better and by what margin?
Is the winner statistically significant or just random chance?
What subject line elements drove higher opens?
How should future campaigns apply these learnings?
payload.narration_text:
A/B test results show you which campaign variant won and why.

The lift percentage is calculated by taking the winner's rate minus the loser's rate, divided by the loser's rate, times 100. This tells you how much better the winner performed.

For reliable results, look for 95% confidence or higher. With less confidence, the difference might just be random chance.

Compare the subject lines carefully. Often small changes like urgency words, emojis, or personalization make the difference. Look at both Open Rate for subject line impact and Click Rate for content impact.

Document what worked - these learnings compound over time to dramatically improve your email performance.

payload.formula_text:
Open Rate = (Unique Opens / Emails Sent) x 100
Click Rate = (Unique Clicks / Emails Sent) x 100
CTOR = (Unique Clicks / Unique Opens) x 100
payload.formula_explanation: These core email metrics measure engagement at different stages of the funnel. Open Rate shows initial interest, Click Rate shows action taken, and Click-to-Open Rate (CTOR) measures content quality by showing what percentage of openers actually clicked.
how_to_read: Green indicator means the metric is performing above the 30-day average
Red indicator means the metric is underperforming compared to benchmark
Sparkline trends show performance across recent campaigns
Compare current values to historical averages to spot anomalies
CTOR is the most important content quality indicator
decisions_answered: How did this campaign perform compared to historical benchmarks?
Is the subject line driving opens effectively?
Is the email content compelling enough to drive clicks?
Should we adjust our content strategy based on CTOR?
payload.narration_text:
The Campaign Performance KPIs give you a snapshot of how well this email engaged your audience.

Open Rate is unique opens divided by emails sent times 100. It tells you how effective your subject line and sender name were at getting people to open.

Click Rate is unique clicks divided by emails sent times 100. This shows what percentage of all recipients took action.

The most valuable metric here is CTOR - Click-to-Open Rate - which is unique clicks divided by unique opens times 100. This isolates content performance from subject line performance.

Green means above your 30-day average. Red means below. Watch the sparklines to see if trends are improving or declining across campaigns.

payload.formula_text:
Delivery Rate = ((Sent - Bounced) / Sent) x 100
Bounce Rate = (Bounced / Sent) x 100
Spam Rate = (Spam Reports / Sent) x 100
payload.formula_explanation: Deliverability health determines whether your emails reach the inbox. High bounce rates indicate list quality issues. High spam rates can damage domain reputation and future deliverability.
how_to_read: Delivery Rate gauge shows overall inbox delivery health
Green zone (98%+) indicates excellent deliverability
Yellow zone (95-98%) requires monitoring
Red zone (below 95%) requires immediate action
Spam rate above 0.1% is a critical warning sign
decisions_answered: Are my emails reaching subscriber inboxes?
Is my list quality degrading over time?
Am I at risk of domain reputation damage?
Do I need to run a list cleaning campaign?
payload.narration_text:
Deliverability metrics reveal whether your emails actually reach people.

Delivery Rate is your most important health indicator. Above 98% is excellent. Between 95 and 98%, watch closely. Below 95% requires immediate attention.

Bounce rate shows list quality. Hard bounces are invalid emails that should be removed. Soft bounces might be temporary issues. Above 2% bounce rate signals list hygiene problems.

Spam rate is critical. Above 0.1% and inbox providers start to penalize your domain. This damages deliverability for ALL future emails.

If you see warning signs, consider a re-engagement campaign to clean your list before it impacts your sender reputation.

payload.formula_text: Hourly Events = COUNT(DISTINCT events) grouped by hour since send
payload.formula_explanation: This chart tracks how engagement unfolds over the 72 hours after send. Most engagement happens in the first 24 hours, with a long tail afterward. The shape reveals audience behavior patterns.
how_to_read: Steep initial peak indicates strong immediate engagement
Green line shows opens over time since send
Purple line shows clicks over time since send
Peak engagement hour reveals optimal send time insights
Long tail indicates ongoing engagement from delayed openers
decisions_answered: When does most engagement happen after sending?
What is the optimal send time for this audience?
How long does engagement typically continue?
Is there a secondary engagement peak at certain hours?
payload.narration_text:
The engagement timeline shows how your audience interacted with this campaign over 72 hours post-send.

The green line tracks opens, the purple line tracks clicks. Most activity happens in the first 6 to 12 hours, then tapers off.

Look for peak engagement hour - this tells you when your audience is most active. If peak is at 2 PM, consider sending future campaigns around that time.

A steep initial curve means strong immediate engagement. A gradual curve might mean people are opening later, perhaps on mobile in the evening.

The gap between opens and clicks shows how well content converts openers to clickers.

payload.formula_text: Country Share = (Country Recipients / Total Recipients) x 100
payload.formula_explanation: This visualization shows where your subscribers are located. Understanding geographic distribution helps optimize send times for different time zones and reveals opportunities for location-specific content.
how_to_read: Larger slices indicate more recipients in that country
Hover for exact recipient counts and percentages
Consider time zones when planning send times
International audiences may need localized content
Use table view for detailed country-by-country metrics
decisions_answered: Where are my subscribers concentrated geographically?
Should I adjust send times for different time zones?
Are there opportunities for location-specific campaigns?
Which regions have the highest engagement rates?
payload.narration_text:
Geographic distribution shows where your subscribers live around the world.

Larger slices mean more recipients in that country. For most businesses, domestic subscribers dominate, but international audiences can be valuable.

Consider time zones when you see significant international presence. A 10 AM send in New York is evening in Europe and middle of the night in Asia.

Compare engagement rates across regions. Some countries might have higher open rates due to cultural factors or send time alignment.

If you have substantial audiences in different regions, consider sending at times optimized for each group, or creating region-specific content that resonates with local interests.

payload.formula_text: Metrics calculated per campaign using standard email formulas
payload.formula_explanation: Comparing the current campaign to recent similar campaigns reveals performance trends and helps identify what makes certain campaigns succeed or fail.
how_to_read: Current campaign is highlighted for easy identification
Sort by any column to rank campaigns on that metric
Green values indicate strong performance on that metric
Red values indicate underperformance requiring investigation
Click any row to navigate to that campaign for details
decisions_answered: How does this campaign compare to recent sends?
What patterns emerge across successful campaigns?
Which campaigns should I analyze for best practices?
Is overall email performance trending up or down?
payload.narration_text:
Historical comparison lets you benchmark this campaign against recent sends.

The current campaign is highlighted so you can easily spot it. Sort by any column to find top and bottom performers.

Look for patterns. Do your best campaigns share common traits like certain subject line styles, send days, or content types?

Green values indicate strong metrics. Red values flag underperformance. When you see red, dig into why - was it the timing, the content, or the audience?

Click any row to jump to that campaign and analyze what worked or what went wrong. Build a mental library of what resonates with your audience.

payload.formula_text:
Revenue per Email = GA4 Revenue / Emails Sent
Revenue per Click = GA4 Revenue / Unique Clicks
payload.formula_explanation: GA4 attribution tracks how much revenue was generated within 7 days of the campaign send. This connects email engagement to actual business outcomes.
how_to_read: Sessions show how many website visits the campaign drove
Revenue shows total attributed sales from the campaign
Revenue per Email (RPE) indicates overall campaign efficiency
Engagement Rate shows quality of traffic from the campaign
Daily bars show revenue distribution over the 7-day window
decisions_answered: How much revenue did this campaign generate?
What is the financial return on this email send?
How engaged was the traffic this campaign drove?
When does most purchase activity occur after the send?
payload.narration_text:
Revenue attribution connects email engagement to actual sales using Google Analytics 4 data.

Sessions tell you how many website visits came from this campaign. Revenue shows total sales attributed within a 7-day window after sending.

Revenue per Email is your efficiency metric - divide total revenue by emails sent. This helps compare campaigns of different sizes on equal footing.

The daily bars show when purchases happened. Most conversions typically occur in the first 24 to 48 hours, but some products have longer consideration cycles.

Use this to identify your highest-revenue campaigns and understand what content and timing drives purchases, not just opens and clicks.

payload.formula_text:
Segment Open Rate = (Segment Opens / Segment Recipients) x 100
Segment Click Rate = (Segment Clicks / Segment Recipients) x 100
payload.formula_explanation: Breaking down performance by audience segment reveals which subscriber groups engage most. This enables targeted content strategies and helps identify underperforming segments that may need different messaging.
how_to_read: Blue bars show Open Rate by segment
Green bars show Click Rate by segment
Longer bars indicate higher engagement from that segment
Compare segment performance to campaign average
Top segments may warrant exclusive content or offers
decisions_answered: Which audience segments engage most with this content?
Are there segments that need different messaging?
Should we create segment-specific versions of future campaigns?
Which segments justify more personalized content investment?
payload.narration_text:
Segment performance breaks down how different audience groups responded to this campaign.

Blue bars show open rates by segment. Green bars show click rates. Longer bars mean higher engagement.

Look for segments that dramatically outperform or underperform the average. High performers might justify exclusive content or early access offers. Low performers might need different messaging angles.

Climate zones show geographic segments. You might find warm climate subscribers engage more with spring planting content while cold climate subscribers prefer indoor gardening tips.

Use these insights to create more targeted, relevant campaigns that resonate with each segment's unique interests.
